title: Tinnitus impact review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports audiological assessment, selected tympanometry, neurological or otological context, and differentiated imaging review for pulsatile, unilateral, asymmetric, or otherwise concerning tinnitus.
examination.rationale: The exact investigations section supports reviewing existing audiology, tympanometry, imaging or specialist results without ordering tests.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports audiological assessment, selected tympanometry, neurological or otological context, and differentiated imaging review for pulsatile, unilateral, asymmetric, or otherwise concerning tinnitus.

assessment.text: Supports documentation of quality-of-life, sleep, concentration, mental-health, behavioural, and psychological impact.
assessment.rationale: The exact impact section supports documenting quality-of-life, sleep, concentration, mental-health and school or work impact plus clinician-performed assessment.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports documentation of quality-of-life, sleep, concentration, mental-health, behavioural, and psychological impact.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.10 — functional, sleep, and psychological impact

treatment_and_management.text: Supports tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, noise and stress context, hearing association, sleep impact, management discussion, individual preferences, information, and questions.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact section supports interval tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, hearing and noise context, sleep impact, preferences and discussion without asserting a cause. The support section allows recording a jointly documented management discussion, follow-up, and questions without creating advice.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, noise and stress context, hearing association, sleep impact, management discussion, individual preferences, information, and questions.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.5 — experience, impact, support, and information

additions.text: Supports tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, noise and stress context, hearing association, sleep impact, management discussion, individual preferences, information, and questions.
additions.rationale: The exact section supports interval tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, hearing and noise context, sleep impact, preferences and discussion without asserting a cause. The support section allows recording a jointly documented management discussion, follow-up, and questions without creating advice.
additions.evidence_extract: Supports tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, noise and stress context, hearing association, sleep impact, management discussion, individual preferences, information, and questions.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.5 — experience, impact, support, and information

additions.text: Supports documentation of quality-of-life, sleep, concentration, mental-health, behavioural, and psychological impact.

additions.evidence_extract: Supports documentation of quality-of-life, sleep, concentration, mental-health, behavioural, and psychological impact.

additions.text: Supports audiological assessment, selected tympanometry, neurological or otological context, and differentiated imaging review for pulsatile, unilateral, asymmetric, or otherwise concerning tinnitus.

source_limitations: Exact tinnitus type, laterality, pulsatility, mental-health risk, test results, diagnosis, and management remain clinician-entered.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
